questions: I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. A coffee shop I've never been to has a specialty drink on their menu I've never heard of. What do I order?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. A friend asks if I want to join an improv class that starts next week. I've never done improv before. What do I say?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. I see a free street performance happening in the park nearby. I wasn't planning to go out today. What do I do?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. There's a new flavor of ice cream at my usual spot that I've never tried before. What do I get?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. I find a new podcast I've never heard of and the first episode is an hour long. What do I do?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. A coworker says there's a free lunch-hour concert in the plaza today — musicians I've never heard of. What do I do?

I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. A colleague says a new Thai restaurant opened nearby and asks if I want to try it for lunch. I've never eaten there. What do I do?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. I see a bookshop is hosting a free author reading tonight for a novelist I've never read. What do I do?

I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. I see an open mic poetry night happening tonight at a local café. I've written poems but never shared them publicly. What do I do?
I act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows me down. There's a new walking trail near my neighborhood I haven't explored before. I have an hour free this afternoon. What do I do?
